codeReview.summary: The deployment-verification feature successfully verified and fixed GitHub Pages deployment for the VitePress documentation site. The worker identified and resolved two critical issues: (1) GitHub Pages was not enabled - fixed via GitHub API, (2) VitePress base path configuration caused duplicate /shellport/ prefix in navigation links - fixed by removing manual /shellport/ prefix from sidebar and nav link definitions in config.ts and index.md since VitePress's base: '/shellport/' setting automatically prepends the base path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used 'gh api repos/igorls/shellport/pages -X POST' to enable GitHub Pages via the GitHub API. This command is not documented in .factory/services.yaml, which only contains basic git and build commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'gh api repos/igorls/shellport/pages -X POST -f build_type=workflow -f source[branch]=main' to enable GitHub Pages. The AGENTS.md mentions GitHub Pages deployment but doesn't document the API commands needed to configure it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md documents 'docs:build', 'docs:dev', 'docs:preview' scripts using 'vitepress' directly (bunx vitepress), but package.json actually uses 'vitepress' without bunx prefix for docs:build. The worker ran 'bun run docs:build' which works correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml has 'docs:build': 'bunx vitepress build docs' but package.json has 'docs:build': 'vitepress build docs'. The worker used 'bun run docs:build' which resolves correctly via package.json script.

summary: The deployment-verification feature passed review. The worker verified GitHub Pages deployment end-to-end: confirmed GitHub Actions workflow exists at .github/workflows/deploy-docs.yml, successfully triggered and monitored workflow runs, enabled GitHub Pages via API (was not pre-configured), verified site is accessible at https://igorls.github.io/shellport/ returning HTTP 200, confirmed multiple pages load correctly (installation.html, basic-usage.html), validated navigation works via browser automation (home to installation page navigation confirmed), mobile responsive flow tested at 375x667 viewport, search functionality verified with encryption query returning relevant results, and no console errors detected. Two issues were identified and fixed during verification: GitHub Pages not enabled (fixed via gh api), and duplicate /shellport/ prefix in nav links (fixed by removing manual prefixes since base: '/shellport/' already handles this). No blocking issues remain.
